name: Process/Event Alarm
description: This includes a list of any process alarms or alerts produced to indicate unusual or concerning activity within an operational process (e.g., increased temperature, pressure, flow rate, or valve status). Unlike IT process alarms (software execution), these alarms typically originate from Industrial Control Systems (ICS), SCADA environments, or physical sensors monitoring Critical Infrastructure.

hmi_scada.description: Logs generated by Human-Machine Interfaces when process variables exceed defined thresholds (e.g., Tank Level > 90%).

historian.description: Time-series database recording sensor values; can be queried for rapid rates of change or limit excursions.

plc_rtu.description: Internal diagnostic buffers or syslog messages sent by Programmable Logic Controllers indicating hardware faults or logic trips.

modbus_tcp.description: Detects protocol-level errors (e.g., Illegal Function, Slave Device Failure) indicating device malfunction or tampering.

dnp3.description: Alerts sent autonomously by outstations to report critical state changes.

sensors.description: IoT or direct-attached sensor feeds indicating physical anomalies.

log_sources.Name: ics:plc_app
log_sources.Channel: OpenPLC detects process state / safety deviation

log_sources.Name: ics:sim_process
log_sources.Channel: Simulation logs process event / alarm state

log_sources.Name: ics:modbus_io
log_sources.Channel: Modbus I/O daemon logs process/event alarm from field devices

log_sources.Name: hmi:catalina
log_sources.Channel: SCADA-LTS raises process/event alarm to operator view
